title: adesmia triphylla
intro: Adesmia triphylla, also known as Queñua or Ecuador Queñua, is a plant species native to the Andes mountain range in South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. Additionally, the plant's bark and roots are sometimes used to make a decoction or tincture, which can be used to treat more severe conditions, such as high blood pressure and arthritis.
